calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-17-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alina Laguna for and with (petitioner) Rosalinda Rubio-Diloreto).

The Court stated that it is missing original signatures on documents, Ms. Laguna stated that she has them with her and the Court instructed her to e-file them. Ms. Laguna stated that she held on to the original signature pages because every time she files something there is an issue. The Court confirmed that signature pages are needed for the consent to Non-Pro Rata Distribution and the pleadings. Ms. Laguna stated that this has been going on for 5 months and that every time she completes something it is not accepted and that the wording she used was taken from the Rutter Group. The Court stated that once it gets the original signatures the only issue left is the non-interest bearing account. Ms. Laguna stated that the bond company required that there be a joint account in that bank, that they believed the bank to be in San Francisco but then found it is in Santa Rosa which is inconvenient to both the attorney and petitioner and that they believed that this was how it had to be. The Court cited 9652 stating that they need to be in compliance with the requirement for an interest bearing account and that it will look for such in the next accounting. The Court took the accounting petition under submission as of August 17, 2016 giving Ms. Laguna time to e-file the original signatures.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (868 Potrero Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94110)
Off Calendar Aug-26-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alina Laguna for and with (administrator) Rosalinda Rubio-Diloreto.

Ms. Laguna reported that proof of publication was filed and a copy given to the examiner. The sale was confirmed at $1,166,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to receipt/review of the order). An additional bond is required with an amount to be determined.

Judge: Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (868 Potrero Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94110)
Off Calendar Aug-10-2015 Continued To Aug-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alina Gonzalesz Laguna, for and with Petitioner Rosalinda Rubio-Diloreto
Ms. Laguna stated that she was uncertain if the concerns raised by the Propate Examinerneeded a response in the form of a declaration or a pleading. The Court replied that she should submit an amended petition addressing the Probate Examiner's notes. Ms. Laguna asked if the filing of an amended petition would result in a need for republication of notice. The Court answered in the affirmative, and ordered that the publication period be shortened to five days. The matter was continued to August 24, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (868 Potrero Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94110)
Off Calendar Jul-29-2015 Continued To Aug-10-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alina Laguna for the estate.

The Court asked for the time frame for publication. Ms. Laguna stated that the last date would be August 3, 2015, that there is an issue with the calculation of the overbid however she has followed the formula and gets the same amount and that she will send notice to everyone with the new hearing date. The Report of sale and petition to confirm sale of real property was continued to August 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition To Disqualify Probate Attorney
Off Calendar Jun-08-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trang La for (beneficiary) Jaime Rubio. Alina Laguna for and with (respondent/administrator) Rosalinda Rubio-Diloreto.

Ms. La stated that there are allegations of $35,000.00 to $40,000.00 which is not accounted for, that the administrator needs to do an investigation, that Ms. Laguna is a witness, has taken a position in sworn testimony that is adverse to the beneficiary and may be called to testify, that Ms. Laguna also counseled the decedent, that Ms. Laguna is hostile towards her client and that she has failed to counsel the administrator regarding her fiduciary duties as well as filing a late opposition. Ms. Laguna stated that the petition is procedurally faulty in that the petition was not served on the beneficiaries and was otherwise without merit, the debt to be collected is from 2010-2011 and not considered debt to the estate, that they are not able to sell the property which is the only asset of the estate, that they have had to spend funds to answer the petition, that Mr. Rubio refused to accept the original service of the probate matter and that the 4 heirs will share the assets equally. The Petition to Disqualify Probate Attorney is denied.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
